<!-- Generated by spec-lite v0.0.5 | sub-agent: feature | date: 2026-03-07 -->

# Feature: Streaming Flow Fix

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-006

Fix the end-to-end Arena streaming pipeline that currently hangs indefinitely
on the first message. Add three independent timeout layers (LLM call, service,
UI) so the spinner always stops, add heartbeat acknowledgement so the user sees
instant feedback, and ensure errors surface in the UI rather than being swallowed
silently. This is the foundation that unblocks F1/F2 (Free-Form Chat) and F3
(Readable Presentation).

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

> The only schema change in this feature is a single field addition to an
> existing interface. No new entities or storage tables are introduced.

### Modified Interface

**`ArenaChatStreamChunk`** (`packages/core/src/types/arena.ts`):
- `heartbeat?: boolean` â€” When `true`, the chunk carries no content; it is a
  keep-alive ping that confirms the extension host received the request. The UI
  must **not** append `content` from heartbeat chunks; it should use them only
  to reset the UI safety timeout and show a progress indicator.
  - Type: `boolean | undefined`
  - Default: absent (falsy) on all normal content/done/error chunks.

No other type changes. `messageId`, `content`, `done`, `error`, and the
remaining optional fields are unchanged.

### TASK-001: Add `heartbeat` field to `ArenaChatStreamChunk`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/core/src/types/arena.ts`, add the following optional field to the
    `ArenaChatStreamChunk` interface, after the existing `error?` field:
    ```typescript
    /**
     * When true, this is a keep-alive heartbeat with no content.
     * The UI should reset its safety timeout and show a progress indicator
     * but must NOT append `content` (which will be empty) to the message body.
     */
    heartbeat?: boolean;
    ```
  - No other changes in this task.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**: None required (pure interface addition with no runtime logic).

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: The JSDoc comment above documents intent inline.

- **Verify**: Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core build` â€” TypeScript compiles
  without errors. Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared build` â€” re-exports
  propagate correctly.

### TASK-002: Add 60 s LLM timeout to `waveClientAgent`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/arena/src/agents/waveClientAgent.ts`, extract a shared helper
    inside `createWaveClientAgent` (not exported):
    ```typescript
    /**
     * Invokes the LLM with a 60 s AbortController timeout.
     * Throws if the LLM call exceeds the deadline.
     */
    async function invokeWithTimeout(
      model: BaseChatModel,
      messages: BaseMessage[],
    ): Promise<BaseMessage> {
      const controller = new AbortController();
      const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 60_000);
      try {
        return await model.invoke(messages, { signal: controller.signal } as RunnableConfig);
      } finally {
        clearTimeout(timer);
      }
    }
    ```
  - Replace the bare `llmWithTools.invoke(messages)` call in `agentNode` with
    `invokeWithTimeout(llmWithTools as BaseChatModel, messages)`.
  - Replace the bare `llm.invoke(messages)` call in `generateNode` with
    `invokeWithTimeout(llm, messages)`.
  - The `catch` block in the public `chat()` generator already propagates errors
    as `ChatChunk` with `error` set â€” no further changes needed there.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (in `packages/vscode/src/test/services/ArenaService.test.ts`
  or a new `packages/arena/src/test/agents/waveClientAgent.test.ts` if it does
  not yet exist):
  - `'agentNode throws when LLM call exceeds 60 s'` â€” mock the LLM to never
    resolve; advance fake timers by 60 001 ms; assert the `chat()` generator
    yields a chunk with `error` set and `done: true`.
  - `'agentNode resolves normally when LLM responds within timeout'` â€” mock LLM
    to resolve in 100 ms; assert normal content chunk is yielded.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add inline JSDoc to `invokeWithTimeout` explaining
  the 60 s default and that the timer is always cleared.

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena build` compiles clean. Unit tests
  for the timeout case pass with `vi.useFakeTimers()`.

### TASK-004: Add heartbeat emission and 90 s stream timeout to `ArenaService`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/arena/src/ArenaService.ts`, in `streamChat()`, immediately
    **before** entering the `for await` loop, emit a heartbeat chunk:
    ```typescript
    onChunk({ messageId, content: '', done: false, heartbeat: true });
    ```
  - Wrap the `for await` loop in a `Promise.race` with a 90 s timeout that
    emits an error chunk and sets `finished = true`:
    ```typescript
    let streamTimedOut = false;
    const streamTimer = setTimeout(() => {
      streamTimedOut = true;
      onChunk({ messageId, content: '', error: 'Request timed out after 90 s', done: true });
    }, 90_000);

    try {
      for await (const chunk of gen) {
        if (signal?.aborted || streamTimedOut) { break; }
        // ... existing chunk handling logic ...
      }
    } finally {
      clearTimeout(streamTimer);
    }

    if (!finished && !streamTimedOut) {
      onChunk({ messageId, content: '', done: true });
    }
    ```
  - Add structured INFO-level logging at stream start and completion:
    ```typescript
    console.info('[Arena] streamChat start', { provider: providerConfig.provider, model: providerConfig.model, agent: request.agent, sessionId: request.sessionId });
    // after loop:
    console.info('[Arena] streamChat complete', { sessionId: request.sessionId, chunkCount, contentLength: accContent.length });
    ```
  - Add WARN-level logging when the stream timeout fires:
    ```typescript
    console.warn('[Arena] stream timeout after 90 s', { sessionId: request.sessionId });
    ```
  - Track `chunkCount` by incrementing a local variable on every `onChunk` call.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (add to `packages/vscode/src/test/services/ArenaService.test.ts`):
  - `'emits heartbeat chunk before first content chunk'` â€” assert first emitted
    chunk has `heartbeat: true`, `content: ''`, `done: false`.
  - `'emits error chunk and returns after 90 s timeout'` â€” mock agent `chat()` to
    return a generator that never yields; advance fake timers by 90 001 ms;
    assert `onChunk` was called with `{ error: 'Request timed out after 90 s', done: true }`.
  - `'clears stream timer on normal completion'` â€” verify no spurious error chunk
    after a normally-completing stream when timers are advanced past 90 s.
  - `'AbortSignal cancellation exits the loop mid-stream'` â€” existing coverage
    exists; verify it still passes.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update JSDoc on `streamChat()` to document the
  heartbeat and 90 s timeout behaviour, and that `onChunk` is always called at
  least once (heartbeat).

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena build`. Unit tests pass.
  Depends on: TASK-001 (heartbeat field on `ArenaChatStreamChunk`).

### TASK-005: Improve `MessageHandler` logging and send heartbeat on receipt

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts`,
    in `handleArenaStreamMessage()`:
    1. Immediately after storing the `AbortController`, send a heartbeat chunk to
       the webview **before** calling `svc.streamChat()`. This gives the UI
       instant feedback that the backend received the request:
       ```typescript
       this.postMessage({
         type: 'arena.streamChunk',
         streamId,
         chunk: { messageId: '', content: '', done: false, heartbeat: true },
       });
       ```
       Note: `messageId` is not yet known at this point (it is assigned inside
       `ArenaService`). Use an empty string â€” the UI must not rely on `messageId`
       for heartbeat chunks.
    2. Upgrade the existing `console.info('[Arena] stream start', ...)` to include
       `sessionId: chatRequest.sessionId`.
    3. Add a `console.debug('[Arena] streamChunk', { streamId, chunkIndex })` call
       inside the `onChunk` callback (track `chunkIndex` with a closure counter).
       Use `console.debug` to avoid flooding INFO logs.
    4. Log stream completion at INFO: `console.info('[Arena] streamComplete', { streamId, sessionId })`.
    5. Upgrade the existing `console.error('[Arena] stream error', ...)` to include
       `sessionId: chatRequest.sessionId`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (add to `packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandler.test.ts`):
  - `'sends heartbeat chunk immediately on arena.streamMessage receipt'` â€” spy on
    `panel.webview.postMessage`; trigger `arena.streamMessage`; assert first
    `postMessage` call is `{ type: 'arena.streamChunk', chunk: { heartbeat: true } }`
    before the streaming response arrives.
  - `'logs stream start and complete at INFO'` â€” spy on `console.info`; assert
    `[Arena] stream start` and `[Arena] streamComplete` entries contain `streamId`
    and `sessionId`.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update the JSDoc on `handleArenaStreamMessage`
  to describe the heartbeat-first protocol.

- **Verify**: Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/vscode build`. Tests pass.
  Depends on: TASK-001.

### TASK-006: Add 120 s UI safety timeout to `vsCodeArenaAdapter.streamMessage`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeArenaAdapter.ts`, in the
    `streamMessage()` method inside the `adapter` object:
    1. After creating the `streamId` and registering listeners in `activeStreams`,
       start a 120 s safety timer:
       ```typescript
       let safetyTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;

       function resetSafetyTimer() {
         if (safetyTimer) clearTimeout(safetyTimer);
         safetyTimer = setTimeout(() => {
           const listeners = activeStreams.get(streamId);
           if (listeners) {
             listeners.errorCbs.forEach((cb) => cb('Stream timed out â€” no response received within 120 s'));
             activeStreams.delete(streamId);
           }
           vsCodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'arena.cancelChat', streamId });
         }, 120_000);
       }

       resetSafetyTimer();
       ```
    2. In the `arena.streamChunk` case of `handleStreamMessage`, call
       `resetSafetyTimer()` to reset the timer on each incoming chunk (including
       heartbeat chunks).
    3. When `arena.streamComplete` or `arena.streamError` is received, clear the
       timer:
       ```typescript
       if (safetyTimer) { clearTimeout(safetyTimer); safetyTimer = null; }
       ```
    4. Build the `StreamHandle` returned by `streamMessage()` as before. Add a
       `cancel()` that includes clearing the safety timer.
  - The `resetSafetyTimer` closure must be scoped per-stream (each call to
    `streamMessage()` creates its own timer).

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (add to `packages/vscode/src/test/adapters/vsCodeArenaAdapter.test.ts`):
  - `'safety timeout fires errorCb after 120 s of silence'` â€” use
    `vi.useFakeTimers()`; call `streamMessage()`; do NOT simulate any incoming
    `arena.streamChunk`; advance timers by 120 001 ms; assert error callback was
    called with the timeout message.
  - `'safety timer resets on each incoming chunk'` â€” receive a chunk at 119 s;
    advance to 239 s (119 + 120); assert error callback was called only after the
    second gap, not the first.
  - `'safety timer is cleared on streamComplete'` â€” simulate `arena.streamComplete`;
    advance past 120 s; assert no error callback is fired.
  - `'safety timer is cleared on streamError'` â€” simulate `arena.streamError`;
    advance past 120 s; assert duplicate error callback is NOT fired.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update the module-level JSDoc on
  `createVSCodeArenaAdapter` to document the 120 s safety timeout and
  heartbeat-reset behaviour.

- **Verify**: Tests pass with `vi.useFakeTimers()`. Depends on: TASK-001.

### TASK-007: Handle heartbeat chunks and guarantee streaming cleanup in `ArenaPane`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/core/src/components/arena/ArenaPane.tsx`, in the `handle.onChunk`
    callback inside `handleSendMessage`:
    1. Skip content append for heartbeat chunks â€” they carry empty `content` by
       design, but be explicit:
       ```typescript
       handle.onChunk((chunk) => {
         if (chunk.heartbeat) {
           // Heartbeat: backend acknowledged the request. No content to append.
           // The safety timeout in vsCodeArenaAdapter already resets automatically.
           return;
         }
         if (chunk.error) {
           updateArenaMessage(assistantMessage.id, {
             status: 'error',
             error: chunk.error,
           });
           return;
         }
         appendArenaStreamingContent(chunk.content);
       });
       ```
    2. In `handle.onError`, ensure `setArenaIsStreaming(false)` is always called
       **before** `notification.showNotification` (current code does this â€” verify
       it is not changed).
    3. In `handle.onDone`, ensure `setArenaIsStreaming(false)` is always called
       first.
    4. Add cleanup in the unmount effect (already present via `activeStreamRef`):
       verify `activeStreamRef.current?.cancel()` is called in the cleanup
       function. If not present, add it.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (create `packages/core/src/test/components/arena/ArenaPane.test.tsx`):
  - `'heartbeat chunk does not append content to streaming message'` â€” render
    `ArenaPane` with `createMockAdapter()`; trigger `handleSendMessage`; simulate
    a `onChunk` call with `{ heartbeat: true, content: '', done: false }`; assert
    that `appendArenaStreamingContent` store action was NOT called (spy on store).
  - `'error chunk sets message status to error and stops streaming'` â€” simulate
    `onChunk` with `{ error: 'Test error', content: '', done: true }`; assert
    `arenaIsStreaming` becomes `false` and message status is `'error'`.
  - `'streaming state is cleaned up on onError'` â€” simulate `onError('Cancelled')`;
    assert `arenaIsStreaming` is `false` after the callback.
  - `'streaming state is cleaned up on onDone'` â€” simulate `onDone(response)`;
    assert `arenaIsStreaming` is `false`.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add inline comment above the `onChunk` callback
  noting the heartbeat fast-path.

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test`. All four new tests pass.
  Depends on: TASK-001.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A â€” this feature operates on in-flight stream sessions; no auth
  changes required.

- **Error Handling**: All timeout events produce explicit error chunks or error
  callbacks. No silent failures. Errors always flow to the UI via
  `notification.showNotification('error', message)` in `ArenaPane`. The Result
  pattern is not directly applicable to streaming (callbacks, not return values),
  but the contract is equivalent: every stream either completes (`onDone`) or
  errors (`onError`), never hangs.

- **Logging**: Per the plan's logging requirements:
  - `[Arena] streamChat start` â€” INFO (provider, model, agent, sessionId).
  - `[Arena] streamChunk` â€” DEBUG (streamId, chunkIndex).
  - `[Arena] streamComplete` â€” INFO (streamId, sessionId, duration, chunkCount).
  - `[Arena] stream timeout after 90 s` â€” WARN (sessionId).
  - `[Arena] stream error` â€” ERROR (streamId, sessionId, error message).
  - `[Arena] LLM timeout after 60 s` â€” WARN (agent, node â€” emitted inside the
    `invokeWithTimeout` helper via `AbortController`'s abort signal; the error
    propagates to the `catch` in `chat()` which logs at ERROR via the chunk).
